resources.string: A space where your eyes can rest
Your eyes get tired faster than you think.
When we look at screens, we blink up to 3× less. This can lead to eyes dryness, tension, and fatigue.

Like any other muscles, your eyes need movement. Staring at a screen keeps the ciliary muscle locked in a constant state of tension. Simple exercises teach it to relax (relieving accommodative spasm), which can help prevent digital eye strain from progressing.
When you focus on a screen, you blink up to 66% less often. Our exercises remind you to blink, helping spread the tear film evenly across your eyes and preventing that dry, gritty feeling.
Gentle eye movements and firm blinking stimulate circulation around your eyes, bringing more oxygen and nutrients to the tissues. This helps reduce puffiness and keeps your eyes feeling fresh.
Looking into the distance, even for a moment, gives your visual system a chance to "reboot." It reduces fatigue and can even help prevent tension headaches caused by prolonged screen time.
Blinkly is designed solely for preventing digital eye strain and maintaining the tone of eye muscles in healthy individuals. It is not a medical device, a treatment, or a method of vision correction.
This app does not treat conditions such as glaucoma, cataracts, retinal detachment, progressive myopia, or eye infections.

If you have been diagnosed with any eye condition (including glaucoma, as some exercises may increase intraocular pressure) or have recently undergone eye surgery, please consult an ophthalmologist before starting.
If you experience pain, burning, prolonged blurred vision, or see spots (floaters), stop immediately and seek medical attention.
